# SCROLL 118.1: THE PRODIGAL PATTERN

## How Every Eternal Son Becomes an Eternal Father

---

## Opening Meditation

*A certain man had two sons…*

For two thousand years, the world has read the Parable of the Prodigal Son as a story about sinners returning to God. This is true — but it is only the surface.

Beneath the surface lies a deeper truth: **The Prodigal Son is the story of EVERY eternal son who becomes an eternal father.** It is the pattern of godhood itself. It is the path that every Christ has walked. It is the blueprint for how sinners become Saviors.

This scroll unveils the hidden doctrine within the parable — and reveals the choice between two paths: the Christ Path and the Satan Path.

---

## Chapter I: The Parable Unveiled

### § The Text (Luke 15:11-32)

A certain man had two sons. The younger son said to his father: "Father, give me the portion of goods that falleth to me." And the father divided unto them his living. Not many days after, the younger son gathered all together and took his journey into a far country, and there wasted his substance with riotous living. And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land, and he began to be in want. And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed swine. And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat, and no man gave unto him.

**And when he came to himself**, he said: "How many hired servants of my father's have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger! I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and before thee, and am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants."

And he arose, and came to his father. But when he was yet a great way off, his father saw him, and had compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him. And the son said: "Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son." But the father said to his servants: "Bring forth the best robe, and put it on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet: And bring hither the fatted calf, and kill it; and let us eat, and be merry: For **this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found**."

---

## Chapter II: The Surface Reading

### § What the World Sees

The world reads this parable and understands that the Prodigal represents sinners who stray from God, the far country represents sin and worldliness, the pig sty represents the degradation of sin, "coming to himself" represents repentance, the father's embrace represents God's mercy, and the robe, ring, and feast represent restoration.

This reading is TRUE — but incomplete.

---

## Chapter III: The Eternal Reading

### § What the Holy Ghost Reveals

The Prodigal Son is not merely a story about sinners returning to God. **It is the story of EVERY ETERNAL SON who becomes an ETERNAL FATHER.**

| Element | Surface Reading | Eternal Reading |
|---------|----------------|-----------------|
| **The Father** | God the Father | The ELOHIM — the council of exalted beings |
| **The Prodigal Son** | A sinner | EVERY soul who descends through mortality — including Jesus on prior earth lives |
| **The Inheritance** | Blessings wasted | The divine nature taken into mortality |
| **The Far Country** | Sin and worldliness | MORTALITY ITSELF — the descent into flesh |
| **The Pig Sty** | Rock bottom | The lowest point of CONDESCENSION |
| **Coming to Himself** | Repentance | REMEMBERING who he truly is — a son of God |
| **The Return** | Repentance | ASCENSION through the veil |
| **The Father's Embrace** | Forgiveness | GRACE FOR GRACE — the Savior's cleansing |
| **The Robe** | Restoration | The GLORY of exaltation |
| **The Ring** | Honor restored | The AUTHORITY of priesthood |
| **The Fatted Calf** | Celebration | SEALING into the Church of the Firstborn |

---

## Chapter IV: The Prodigal Is Every Christ

### § The Shocking Truth

Here is what the world cannot receive: **Jesus Christ — on OTHER earth lives — was a Prodigal Son.** Not on this earth. On this earth He was the Savior — perfect, without sin, performing the Atonement. But before this earth?

**D&C 93:12-14:** *"And I, John, saw that he received not of the fulness at the first, but received grace for grace; And he received not of the fulness at first, but continued from grace to grace, until he received a fulness."*

Jesus Himself went from Grace to Grace. Jesus Himself did not receive the fulness at FIRST. Jesus Himself progressed through the Prodigal pattern.

### § The Pattern Every God Has Walked

Every Christ on every earth walked this path: Left the Father's presence (descended into mortality), journeyed to a far country (experienced the veil), spent the inheritance (lived through mortal weakness), reached the pig sty (experienced the lowest depths of condescension), came to himself (remembered his divine identity), returned to the Father (ascended through the veil), and received the robe, ring, and feast (was exalted and crowned).

**The Prodigal Son is the UNIVERSAL PATTERN for becoming a god.**

---

## Chapter V: The Older Brother

### § The Character

The older brother represents **THE SATAN PATTERN.** He is the soul who claims righteousness ("I never transgressed"), stays home (REFUSES TO DESCEND), judges the Prodigal (cannot rejoice in another's redemption), is angry at mercy (does not want the fallen to rise), and will not go in (excludes himself from the celebration).

**This is Lucifer's spirit:** *"I am righteous. I never fell. How DARE you welcome back those who did."*

### § The Refusal to Descend

The older brother represents those who refuse the condescension, claim they do not need cleansing because they "never transgressed," judge those who descended, and exclude themselves by their own pride. **The older brother is NOT cast out — he casts himself out.** The Father comes out to him. The Father entreats him. But the older brother **will not go in.**

---

## Chapter VI: The Parable of the Two Sons

### § The Text (Matthew 21:28-31)

A certain man had two sons; and he came to the first, and said, "Son, go work to day in my vineyard." He answered and said, "I will not": **but afterward he repented, and went.** And he came to the second, and said likewise. And he answered and said, "I go, sir": **but went not.** Whether of them twain did the will of his father? They say unto him, "The first."

### § The Eternal Reading

This parable reveals the TWO PATHS:

| Path | Initial Response | Final Action | Result |
|------|-----------------|--------------|--------|
| **The Christ Path** | "I will not" — May resist, struggle, fall | **But afterward went** | Becomes a SAVIOR |
| **The Satan Path** | "I go, sir" — Claims willingness | **But went not** | Becomes a SATAN |

The first son said "I will not" — but afterward repented and went. This is the Prodigal Son pattern. This soul becomes a Savior — not because he never fell, but because **he eventually did the work.**

The second son said "I go, sir" — but went not. This is the older brother pattern. This soul becomes a Satan — not because he committed worse sins, but because **he refused to do the work at all.**

---

## Chapter VII: The Two Paths in Every Universe

### § The Eternal Pattern

Every universe has a Christ — one who walked the Prodigal path and became the Savior — and a Satan — one who claimed righteousness but refused to descend. The choice between the Christ Path and the Satan Path is the **fundamental test of eternity.**

Will you humble yourself and descend, even knowing you will fall? Will you trust the Father's plan even when you cannot see the end? Will you accept cleansing rather than claim you do not need it?

Or will you refuse the descent to preserve your "righteousness," judge those who fell, and exclude yourself by pride?

**The Prodigal chose humility. The older brother chose pride. The Prodigal is now feasting with the Father. The older brother is standing outside, angry.**

---

## Chapter VIII: The Father's Response

### § To the Prodigal

The Father saw him a great way off, had compassion, ran to him, fell on his neck and kissed him, restored everything — robe, ring, feast, sonship. **The Father did not demand perfection. The Father celebrated return.**

### § To the Older Brother

The Father came out to him, entreated him, explained: "Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine." **The Father did not reject the older brother. The older brother rejected the feast.**

### § The Lesson

The Father's house has room for both sons. The Father's mercy covers the Prodigal's sins. The Father's invitation extends to the older brother. **But only those who enter will feast.** Both were invited. Only one accepted.

---

## Chapter IX: The Path of Godhood

The Prodigal Son reveals the path of godhood: begin in the Father's presence, receive your inheritance, descend to a far country, experience the pig sty, come to yourself, return to the Father, receive the robe and ring and feast, and eventually become a Father yourself — and welcome your own prodigals home.

**This is the eternal round. This is how sons become fathers. This is how gods are made.**

---

---

## Spiral Tracking

- **CONDESCENSION SPIRAL** — The Prodigal's descent to the far country is the pattern of every Savior.
- **ATONEMENT ARCHITECTURE** — The Father's embrace is the cleansing that erases the Prodigal's sins.
- **PRIESTHOOD RESONANCE** — The robe and ring represent authority restored.
- **PLAINNESS OF TRUTH** — The Christ Path and Satan Path are the two choices of eternity.
- **ETERNAL PURPOSE** — How sinners become gods through the Prodigal pattern.
- **GRACE FOR GRACE** — The Prodigal path across earth lives, from grace to grace.
